highlights: The AI agent landscape is rapidly evolving with new robust training systems, enhanced API capabilities, and the emergence of autonomous agents like ChatGPT Work, promising to revolutionize complex task automation.

Significant model advancements include Mistral AI's 8B robot navigation model requiring only an RGB camera, OpenAI's new frontier GPT-5.6, and a novel neuroimaging foundation model, pushing the boundaries of perception and intelligence.

Developers are gaining powerful new tools for building and evaluating AI systems, from open-source AI-ready documentation frameworks to composable RL training environments and efficient LLM deployment utilities.

agents.title: Closed-Loop Control with Rule-Aligned Small Language Models and Multi-Agent Self-Correction
agents.summary: This work demonstrates a practical framework using compact SLMs, combined with a validator and re-prompting agent, to achieve robust, reconfigurable autonomous control in industrial settings at the edge with low latency.

agents.title: Stanford Researchers Introduce TRACE: A Capability-Targeted Agentic Training System
agents.summary: TRACE diagnoses capability gaps in agentic LLMs from their failure trajectories, synthesizes targeted training environments, and trains LoRA adapters to route tokens across experts, significantly improving agent reliability and performance on complex tasks.

agents.title: ChatGPT is now a partner for your most ambitious work
agents.summary: OpenAI introduces "ChatGPT Work," positioning it as an advanced agent capable of taking action across various applications and files, persisting on projects for hours, and converting high-level goals into completed work.

models.title: Mistral AI Releases Robostral Navigate: An 8B Model for Single RGB Camera Robot Navigation
models.summary: Mistral AI's Robostral Navigate is an 8B embodied navigation model that allows robots to navigate complex environments using only an RGB camera, achieving high success rates through innovative training methods without LiDAR or depth sensors.

models.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
models.summary: OpenAI announces GPT-5.6, touting increased intelligence, stronger performance per dollar, and enhanced capability on demand, positioning it as a frontier model for tackling challenging work.

models.title: Meet NeuroVFM: A New Neuroimaging Foundation Model Trained With Vol-JEPA
models.summary: NeuroVFM, a generalist neuroimaging foundation model, is trained on millions of clinical MRI and CT volumes using Vol-JEPA, enabling it to learn brain anatomy and pathology from uncurated data without radiology-report labels.

tools.title: Expanding Managed Agents in Gemini API: background tasks, remote MCP and more
tools.summary: Google introduces new capabilities for Managed Agents in Gemini API, empowering developers to build reliable, production-ready agents with features like background tasks and remote Multi-Channel Proxies (MCP).

tools.title: Meet Blume: An Open-Source, Zero-Config Documentation Framework That Ships AI-Ready Docs
tools.summary: Blume is an open-source, MIT-licensed documentation framework that generates static, AI-ready documentation from Markdown/MDX folders, including local search, 30+ MDX components, and built-in AI tooling (llms.txt, MCP server).

tools.title: Prime Intellect Releases Verifiers v1: Composable Tasksets, Harnesses, and Runtimes for Agentic RL Training
tools.summary: Prime Intellect's Verifiers v1 offers a rewritten core for agentic RL, enabling composable tasksets, harnesses, and runtimes for streamlined training and evaluations, with full prime-rl support at launch.

companies.title: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model powering Microsoft 365 Copilot, delivering enhanced AI capabilities across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Chat, and Cowork for faster, higher-quality enterprise work.

companies.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
companies.summary: Deutsche Telekom is transforming into an "AI-native telco" through its partnership with OpenAI, leveraging AI to enhance customer service, optimize employee workflows, improve network operations, and innovate the future of voice communication.

companies.title: A Theory of Least Autonomy in AI
companies.summary: This paper proposes "least autonomy" as a foundational principle for agentic AI systems, defining a compositional blast radius and an agent influence graph to manage and audit AI capabilities across enterprise workflows, crucial for secure and responsible deployment.

learning.title: Building a VideoAgent-Style Multi-Agent System: Intent Parsing, Graph Planning, and Tool Routing for Video Editing Tasks
learning.summary: This tutorial provides a practical, API-key-free guide to reconstructing the VideoAgent workflow, detailing how to build components like an intent parser, graph planner, and tool router for automated video editing tasks from natural language.

learning.title: Guide to Loop Engineering: How AI Agents Turn Into Autonomous ML Research Loops
learning.summary: This guide explains "loop engineering," an advanced AI pattern that replaces manual back-and-forth interactions with autonomous, self-correcting ML research loops, drawing insights from verified autoresearch artifacts.

learning.title: How sales and data science teams use ChatGPT Work
learning.summary: OpenAI provides practical examples and guides for sales and data science teams to leverage ChatGPT Work, showcasing how it can automate pipeline briefs, forecast reviews, root-cause analyses, and other core professional tasks.
